name: Maple Crisps
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch 1/1/1990
1. Cream together butter and granulated sugar using an electric mixer until smooth.
2. Add the egg and maple syrup, mixing until well combined.
3. Mix in the maple extract.
4. Gradually add the sifted flour to the batter, beating thoroughly after each addition.
5. Cover the dough and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
6. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured pastry cloth to about 1/8-inch thickness.
7. Cut into various shapes using cookie cutters.
8. Transfer the cookies to ungreased baking sheets. If desired, sprinkle with coconut before baking (the coconut will adhere without an egg-white wash).
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 6 to 8 minutes or until lightly browned around the outer edges. Remove from the oven and let cool immediately on wire racks.
10. Frost cooled cookies with buttercream frosting, spread with soft cream cheese, and top with candied peel if desired. Yield: Approximately 10 dozen cookies measuring about 1 1/2 inches in diameter. Suggestions include using jam, lemon curd, or chocolate glaze to top the cookies, or creating a sandwich with a sweet filling by joining two cookies together.
